name: Water Molecule (H₂O)
description: A single water molecule. H₂O: molecular weight 18.015 Da. Mass = 18.015 × 1.66054×10⁻²⁷ kg = 2.9907×10⁻²⁶ kg. Contains exactly 2 hydrogen atoms (Z=1, A=1) and 1 oxygen atom (Z=8, A=16): 10 protons, 8 neutrons, 10 electrons. The O–H covalent bond length is 95.84 pm; bond angle 104.45°. Total nuclear binding energy: 2×(0 MeV for H) + 127.6 MeV for O-16 = 127.6 MeV. A benchmark for the smallest load — tests the single-molecule resolution of the pipeline.
